meaning: adj.寰敓鐗╃殑,鐢辩粏鑿屽紩璧风殑
examples: Grazing herds stimulate microbial activity in the soil, helping to capture water and separate carbon.
Previously it was thought there were around 100, 000 microbial cells in every litre of seawater but scientists now think there could be one billion.
Many are found in tropical and subtropical areas of the world, where the accumulation of high concentrations of metals may afford some protection against plant-eating insects and microbial pathogens.
Importantly, the anti-microbial surface remained even when the host material was washed or otherwise agitated.
But some human proteins are either too large or too complex for microbial cells to synthesize.
